items.summary: The annual Par 3 Contest on the eve of the US Masters has produced four aces, but Tommy Fleetwood's eight-year-old son stole the show at Augusta National.

items.title: Australian playwright S Shakthidharan wins $250,000 Windham-Campbell Prize
items.summary: S Shakthidharan, whose plays include The Wrong Gods, The Jungle and the Sea and Counting and Cracking, has won the prestigious Windham-Campbell Prize.

items.title: Geraldton man charged with murder after death of 69yo man sparks homicide investigation
items.summary: A man dies in hospital after an altercation at a home in Padbury, and police have charged a 39-year-old man who was arrested at the scene with murder.

items.title: China's influence on the US-Iran ceasefire deal
items.summary: China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi says he held 26 phone calls with his counterparts in Iran, Israel, Russia and Gulf countries before the ceasefire was reached.

items.title: NT government in talks with Daly River residents, landowners over potential relocation of Nauiyu
items.summary: Flood-affected residents from Daly River are in talks with the NT government about moving the remote Aboriginal community to higher ground, with a church offering to donate land for the township's relocation.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz ship traffic remains at standstill despite Iran, US and Israel ceasefire
items.summary: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has remained at an effective standstill in the 24 hours since Iran conditionally lifted its blockade on the critical shipping lane, amid a fragile ceasefire with the US and Israel.

items.title: Geraldton house vacant for more than a year after retiree renter forced to leave
items.summary: After a lifetime of caring for the elderly, Linda Braam is left battling for a place to call home.

items.title: More than 1,000 fish killed in Darwin Port after iron ore pollution
items.summary: More than 1,000 small fish die in Darwin Port after acidic run-off from an iron ore stockpile enters a containment pond following heavy rain.

items.title: Melbourne Comedy Festival: Grace Jarvis on comedy as an autistic artform, chronic pain and working in a sex shop
items.summary: Grace Jarvis talks about everything from her experiences with autism, ADHD, queerness, and working in a sex shop in her stand-up. Her experiences with chronic illness? Not so much.

items.title: Rolling 2026 AFL All-Australian team 1.0: Max Gawn tracking towards league record as Will Ashcroft headlines eight first-timers
items.summary: Melbourne captain Max Gawn shows no sign of slowing down as an excellent first month of the season puts him front and centre of ABC Sport's rolling AFL All-Australian team.

items.title: WA food charities inundated as fuel crisis sends more people to their doors
items.summary: From shrinking their hampers to closing waitlists, food charities in Western Australia are adapting their services to meet a surge in demand as more people struggle with rising costs.

items.title: Punmu's future at stake as damaged road leaves remote community at 'crisis point'
items.summary: A remote Aboriginal community in Western Australia has six weeks' worth of food remaining and no truck driver is willing to travel down the notorious road to reach it.

items.title: Daniil Medvedev smashes racquets, loses 6-0, 6-0 to Matteo Berrettini at Monte Carlo Masters
items.summary: Daniil Medvedev endures an eventful day at the Monte Carlo Masters, smashing several racquets before suffering the first 6-0, 6-0 trouncing of his distinguished career.

items.title: Darwin's 'dangerous' bus network will soon have officers armed with guns
items.summary: As the conflict in the Middle East continues, more people than ever are turning to public transport to escape the cost of driving — but in Darwin, that choice comes with a different price.

items.title: Nick Faldo says he will not comment on rival Greg Norman until after US Masters
items.summary: Respectfully biting his tongue, Nick Faldo is refusing to dignify fresh criticism from Greg Norman that is certain to reignite one of golf's fiercest rivalries.

items.title: UK man who sexually abused girl has visa reinstated over 'strong ties' to Australia
items.summary: The man's permanent visa was automatically cancelled when he was jailed in 2024 for sexually abusing a nine-year-old girl, but a tribunal has upheld his appeal in part due to his "ill health".

items.title: Australians dust off bikes, buy trikes as fuel prices surge amid Iran war
items.summary: Skyrocketing fuel prices are driving Australians to pedal power, with some bicycle stores and repair services experiencing a surge in demand.

items.title: Calls to review Australian food labelling laws as farmers fight cheap imports
items.summary: Farmers battling cheap imported produce want a review of food labelling laws to highlight what Australian produce is included in products.

items.title: Police investigate fatal shooting of man at Box Hill in Melbourne's east
items.summary: Homicide squad detectives from Victoria Police are investigating a fatal shooting at a Box Hill apartment building.

items.title: AC Milan, Inter Milan and Juventus to play matches in Perth
items.summary: Perth will be the centre of Italian preseason football in August, with some of the game's heavyweights heading to the Western Australian capital ahead of the next European season.

items.title: Fuel price hikes force families to reconsider travel for medical treatment

items.title: FOI reveals extent of Adelaide Writers' Week crisis, including locked doors and threats to staff
items.summary: An extraordinary series of emails sheds light on the reservations that preceded, and internal fallout that followed, the decision to scrap Randa Abdel-Fattah's Writers' Week appearance.

items.title: Neuroscientist slams AFL's 'potentially dangerous' headgear move

items.title: Camperdown couple 'relieved' to see home relocated after being built on wrong block

items.title: Severe Cyclone Maila moving again towards Far North Queensland after stalling in Solomon Sea

items.title: Public pledges sought for Justice For Her to help spark 'actions' to reduce gendered violence rates

items.title: Farmers furious as Victorian government fast-tracks caged hen rule changes
items.summary: The lives of caged hens are set to improve as Victoria implements new welfare laws, but farmers say they have not been consulted.

items.title: NT government urged to release 'secretive' review into Kumanjayi White's death
items.summary: The family of Indigenous man Kumanjayi White has criticised the secrecy around the independent review into his death, which has been handed to NT Police and the DPP but not released publicly.

items.title: 'Ketamine Queen' jailed for 15 years over death of Friends star Matthew Perry

items.title: Mining on the Moon the next space race in the bid to explore the final frontier
items.summary: A scientist, an engineer, an archaeologist and a lawyer break down some of the physical, environmental, ethical and legal challenges of mining on the Moon.

items.title: Anthony Albanese dashes to Singapore as Australia engages in a costly, complex fuel scramble
items.summary: Anthony Albanese is flying to Singapore as part of a hastily arranged regional mission to shore up crucial fuel supplies, amid warnings the global energy crisis could "sharply turn for the worse".

items.title: How long will it take petrol prices to get back to pre-Iran war levels, if at all?
items.summary: Iran's promise to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after striking a temporary agreement with the US saw the cost of oil fall sharply. But how quickly are we likely to see relief at the bowser in Australia? Analysts say it could be weeks or months.

items.title: As Trump's Iran rhetoric escalates, Australia turns to the art of diplomatic understatement
items.summary: Neither side of politics in Australia rushed to enthusiastically celebrate the ceasefire deal that followed US President Donald Trump's threat.

items.title: How the legacy of Australia's Black Power movement continues today
items.summary: When a "bunch of nobodies" in Sydney decided to stand up to police brutality, no-one could guess their impact would still be felt more than 50 years later.

items.title: TV quiz: Match the quote to the classic Australian television show
items.summary: One quote, two possible answers: test your knowledge and take a nostalgic trip down memory lane as you match the quote to the iconic Australian television series.

items.title: The Australian Ballet and Bangarra Dance Theatre unite for Flora after failed referendum
items.summary: Sixteen dancers from Bangarra Dance Theatre and 19 ballet dancers from the Australian Ballet have joined forces for Flora, choreographed by Bangarra artistic director Frances Rings.

items.title: US architect Rex Heuermann admits to murder of eight women in Gilgo Beach case
items.summary: Rex Heuermann pleads guilty to seven murders and admits to killing another woman, solving the notorious case known as the Gilgo Beach killings.

items.title: Sam Williamson, Mollie O'Callaghan, Kaylee McKeown, Lani Pallister, Sam Short claim wins at Australian Open swimming championships
items.summary: Sam Williamson capped his comeback with victory in the 50m breaststroke just 11 months after he suffered a career-threatening injury.

items.title: Australia to remain in talks on action in the Strait of Hormuz as ceasefire takes effect
items.summary: Defence Minister Richard Marles says discussions will continue about Australia's involvement in ensuring the Strait of Hormuz remains open.

items.title: Queensland Rail extends planned train track closures citing protected industrial action by union workers
items.summary: Train commuters across Brisbane will be impacted by an additional four days of track closures with Queensland Rail citing protected industrial action by union workers as the reason for the extension.

items.title: Free public transport luring thousands more Tasmanians onto buses
items.summary: The temporary ability to catch public transport for free is turning some Tasmanians into "converts", while others are using it for new experiences.

items.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: St John Ambulance NT reaches 'critical system failure' for second time in a week, calls for more resources
items.summary: The Northern Territory's ambulances service has reached "critical system failure" for the second time in a week, as a St John NT director says the service is so under-resourced its operations are at risk of failure "all the time".

items.title: Pet dog survives crocodile attack at Lake Argyle in WA's remote Kimberley
items.summary: A pet dog has a lucky escape after being dragged underwater by a freshwater crocodile at Lake Argyle in WA's remote Kimberley.

items.title: Jackie O complained about Kyle Sandilands months before on-air fight, court documents allege
items.summary: Court documents allege Jackie "O" Henderson was subject to "offensive and degrading" comments about her personal and dating life from co-host Kyle Sandilands in two separate incidents last year.

items.title: Bones discovered during bus stop construction work on Rottnest Island, sparking investigation
items.summary: Police are investigating the origin of several human bones that were found on Rottnest Island.

items.title: The far side revealed: Artemis II's lunar fly-by in pictures
items.summary: A record-breaking voyage, an earthset in silence, and a total solar eclipse — astronauts on the Artemis II mission experienced sights no human has witnessed in half a century.

items.title: When is the best time to get your flu shot? Two infectious diseases experts explain
items.summary: When working out the best time to get your vaccine, you may be balancing a theoretical benefit by waiting against the chance of actually getting the flu before you get vaccinated.

items.title: The three reasons behind Penrith's record-breaking start to the 2026 NRL season
items.summary: Already, the 2026 premiership looks like it's Penrith's to lose, and there are three big reasons behind the Panthers' record-breaking start to the season.

items.title: SA farmers gamble on early winter crops after record rain, but expensive fuel and fertiliser a big concern
items.summary: Some SA farmers have rolled the dice on planting winter crops weeks earlier than usual thanks to record-breaking rains. Now they hope the gamble pays off.

items.title: Federal government criticised for response into spread of sea urchins
items.summary: Millions of destructive sea urchins are spreading from NSW into Victoria and Tasmania and the chair of a Senate inquiry says reefs are at risk unless there is decisive action.

items.title: Ken Hinkley says he was shamed and exploited by AFL over controversial fine
items.summary: Ex-Port Adelaide coach Ken Hinkley, who was fined for sledging in 2024, says he felt exploited by the league's later use of footage of his theatrics as a promotional tool.

items.title: Shayna Jack 'lighter' as she strives for Commonwealth Games selection
items.summary: Shayna Jack says her Paris Olympics campaign was fuelled by "anger and hate", but she is back to being herself — "lighter" and with more personal belief than ever.

items.title: Trump's escalating rhetoric inappropriate for a United States president, Albanese says
items.summary: Hours before Donald Trump announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran, the United States president threatened that "a whole civilisation will die tonight, never to be brought back again".

items.title: What is financial stress and how does it affect our decisions?
items.summary: If financial stress is causing distractions — for example, you're worrying about money when you should be concentrating on driving or work — there might be something deeper happening.

items.title: Gary Woodland details PTSD struggles ahead of US Masters
items.summary: In an interview before this week's US Masters, former US Open champion Gary Woodland opens up about his mental health struggles.

items.title: Water storage feud heats up as frustrated Central Queensland farmers take on corporate giants
items.summary: Central Queensland irrigators are demanding a "fair go" from two mega water users that are pumping from a ratepayer-owned storage without paying anything for its multimillion-dollar costs.

items.title: Nursing union says Royal Darwin Hospital staff face violent threats, fear for safety
items.summary: NT Health has rejected calls for a security review at Royal Darwin Hospital's busy emergency department amid threats against staff, with staff concerned "a worker is going to be stabbed".

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith does not apply for bail in first court hearing on war crime charges
items.summary: War veteran Ben Roberts-Smith will remain in custody after his lawyers made no application for bail in his first court hearing since his arrest.

items.title: Mitch Duke to retire from Socceroos after FIFA World Cup bid
items.summary: Mitch Duke will retire from international football this year, but is still hopeful of being part of Australia's FIFA World Cup squad.

items.title: Squid sightings spawn hope in SA amid calls for longer-lasting restrictions
items.summary: Sightings of southern calamari in South Australia are bringing relief after the species all but disappeared from some areas during the harmful algal bloom that began in early 2025.

items.title: Swimming Australia's support dog experiment wins plenty of fans at Australian Open
items.summary: The swimmers at the Australian Open championships this week have had additional support from the side of the pool in the form of three retriever dogs.

items.title: Sydney healthcare workers push for refugee Medicare access amid growing health concerns
items.summary: A growing number of people seeking asylum in Australia are missing out on lifesaving preventative health care due to being ineligible for Medicare, a group of Sydney doctors and nurses say.

items.title: Bryson DeChambeau hoping to continue rivalry with Rory McIlroy at US Masters
items.summary: Bryson DeChambeau has fired the first verbal shot at Rory McIlroy as golf's alpha males prepare to collide in a compelling 90th Masters at Augusta National.

items.title: Phillip Norris walks on new leg
